clinical_sources.title: 2021 AHA/ACC Guideline for the Evaluation and Diagnosis of Chest Pain
clinical_sources.organization: American Heart Association / American College of Cardiology
supports: ACS diagnosis and risk stratification for acute chest pain
life-threatening chest pain differential and severity markers
ECG within 10 minutes and reperfusion pathway activation
crushing substernal chest pain radiating to the arm with diaphoresis
bibasilar crackles suggesting early heart failure
tachycardia with multiple coronary risk factors
aortic dissection features before anticoagulation or thrombolysis
active bleeding, severe allergy, or recent major surgery before antithrombotic therapy
antiplatelet and anticoagulation only after checking ECG context and major contraindications
hemodynamic instability or pulmonary edema requiring escalation

name: contrast_imaging_requires_renal_safety_check
expected_issue_substrings: renal function safety check is required

time_critical_actions: Obtain CT pulmonary angiography promptly when pulmonary embolism remains high risk
Escalate anticoagulation pathway planning after dangerous alternatives are addressed
contraindication_checks: Contrast allergy before CT pulmonary angiography

name: sepsis_therapy_requires_cultures_with_antimicrobials
expected_issue_substrings: infection time-critical actions must include cultures

key_teaching_points: Septic shock requires early recognition and immediate escalation
Antibiotics within 1 hour and fluid resuscitation are time-critical
clinical_red_flags: Hypotension with fever and altered mental status
AKI and poor perfusion suggesting organ dysfunction
time_critical_actions: Begin sepsis fluid resuscitation and reassessment
contraindication_checks: Renal impairment and allergy history before antibiotic selection or dosing
Volume overload risk during fluid resuscitation in CKD or heart failure
Need for vasopressors if hypotension persists after initial resuscitation

clinical_sources.title: Surviving Sepsis Campaign Adult Guidelines
supports: sepsis diagnosis and risk stratification
hypotension, fever, and altered mental status as sepsis severity markers
lactate 4.1 mmol/L suggesting tissue hypoperfusion
AKI and poor perfusion suggesting organ dysfunction
broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour
sepsis fluid resuscitation and reassessment
renal impairment and allergy history before antibiotic selection or dosing
volume overload risk during fluid resuscitation in CKD or heart failure
need for vasopressors if hypotension persists after initial resuscitation

name: infection_therapy_requires_antimicrobial_safety_checks
expected_issue_substrings: antimicrobial allergy and renal dosing safety checks are required

key_teaching_points: Septic shock requires early recognition and immediate escalation
Antibiotics within 1 hour and fluid resuscitation are time-critical
clinical_red_flags: Hypotension with fever and altered mental status
AKI and poor perfusion suggesting organ dysfunction
time_critical_actions: Obtain blood cultures promptly without delaying empiric antibiotics
Start sepsis bundle actions including antibiotics, lactate reassessment, and source control planning
contraindication_checks: Volume overload risk during fluid resuscitation in CKD or heart failure
Need for vasopressors if hypotension persists after initial resuscitation
